verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (HSP70-1/HSP72) is canonically a cytosolic chaperone with no TM domain, GPI anchor, or classical surface-targeting signal. However, robust gene-specific experimental evidence documents tumour-selective plasma membrane display: multi-parameter flow cytometry with a membrane-conformation-specific antibody (cmHsp70.1), selective cell-surface iodination, and global surface-proteomics have all confirmed HSP70/HSPA1A on the outer face of tumour cells. The proposed mechanism involves interaction with intracellular phosphatidylserine and/or glycosphingolipid Gb3, enabling translocation to the outer leaflet in a Brefeldin-A-independent fashion. This surface display is restricted to malignantly transformed (oncogenically stressed) cells and is absent or negligible on normal non-stressed cells — qualifying as oncogenic/stress-state cell_state_induced. The 5-bucket check: (1) cancer ecto-pool — YES, documented for this specific protein; (2) cell-death display — contributes via ICD/necrotic release, but less central; (3) germline-restricted — no evidence; (4) lysosomal exocytosis — not the dominant mechanism here; (5) stable TM-partner tethering — not established (phospholipid binding is reversible, wash-resistance unconfirmed). The dominant mechanism qualifying for contextual is oncogenic-state outer-leaflet translocation (cell_state_induced).
